title: Christopher Marlowe & Doctor Faustus | BA English Hons | Elizabethan Drama in Hindi
description:
This video introduces Christopher Marlowe and his masterpiece Doctor Faustus for BA English Honours students and those preparing for university exams. We explore Marlowe's life, his revolutionary contribution to English drama as the father of blank verse, and the tragic premise of Doctor Faustus — a scholar who sells his soul to the Devil for unlimited power and knowledge. The video is delivered in mixed Hindi-English to make complex literary concepts accessible and exam-ready.

What you'll learn:
• Christopher Marlowe's biography and his rise from modest background to Cambridge scholar
• Why Marlowe is called the Father of Blank Verse and how this transformed English drama
• Marlowe's mysterious life, accusations of atheism, and the enigmatic circumstances of his death in 1593
• The tragic premise of Doctor Faustus: ambition, the Faustian bargain, and the 24-year pact with the Devil
• Key themes of Renaissance humanism versus Christian moral framework
• Why overreaching ambition leads to damnation and the play's enduring moral lesson

Chapters:
0:00 Introduction & Channel Welcome
0:15 Christopher Marlowe's Life & Background
0:50 Marlowe's Literary Style & Legacy
1:25 Marlowe's Dramatic Life & Death
2:10 Doctor Faustus: Plot Summary
2:55 Themes: Ambition & Consequences
